title: scirpus lanatus
intro: Scirpus lanatus, also known as woolgrass or woolly club rush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is widely used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Scirpus lanatus is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional remedies like poultices and salves.
